017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.security.*;
023import java.util.*;
024
025/**
026 * A {@link Principal} that exposes typed read-only access to a map of claims.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * Used by token validators that produce structured assertions about the authenticated subject &mdash;
030 * e.g. JWTs (the {@code juneau-rest-server-auth-jwt} {@code JwtTokenValidator} returns a
031 * {@code ClaimsPrincipal} with the verified token's claim set) or OAuth 2.0 introspection responses.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * The "principal name" returned by {@link #getName()} is supplied at construction time; callers can
035 * pick whichever claim is canonical for their identity model (typically {@code sub}). The full claim
036 * set is reachable via {@link #getClaims()} and per-claim typed lookup via
037 * {@link #getClaim(String, Class)}.
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * Instances are <b>immutable</b> &mdash; the claims map supplied to the constructor is defensively
041 * copied and exposed as an unmodifiable view. {@code ClaimsPrincipal} can be safely cached and
042 * shared across requests.
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>Type coercion contract for {@link #getClaim(String, Class)}:</h5>
045 *
046 * <ul>
047 *    <li>If the underlying value is already an instance of the requested type, it is returned directly.
048 *    <li>If the requested type is {@link String}, the value's {@link Object#toString()} is returned.
049 *    <li>{@link Number} sub-types ({@link Integer}, {@link Long}, {@link Double}) coerce via the
050 *       matching {@code Number.xxxValue()} accessor; non-numeric values yield {@link Optional#empty()}.
051 *    <li>{@link Boolean} coerces from {@link Boolean} only.
052 *    <li>Any other mismatch yields {@link Optional#empty()} rather than a cast exception.
053 * </ul>
054 *
055 * <p>
056 * The coercion table is intentionally conservative &mdash; the validator that built the claims map is
057 * the authoritative source of richer type-mapping. If a JWT validator decodes a claim as a
058 * {@code List<String>}, callers can read it back as {@code List.class} or pull individual
059 * elements via {@link #getClaims()} and operate on the {@link List} directly.
060 *
061 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
062 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Auth}
063 *    <li class='jc'>{@link TokenValidator}
064 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7519#section-4">RFC 7519 &sect;4 &mdash; JWT Claims</a>
065 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerAuthGuards">AuthN Guards</a>
066 * </ul>
067 *
068 * @since 10.0.0
069 */
070public class ClaimsPrincipal implements Principal {
071
072   private final String name;
073   private final Map<String,Object> claims;
074
075   /**
076    * Constructor.
077    *
078    * @param name The principal name. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
079    * @param claims The claim map. May be <jk>null</jk> (treated as an empty map). Defensively copied.
080    */
081   public ClaimsPrincipal(String name, Map<String,Object> claims) {
082      assertArgNotNullOrBlank("name", name);
083      this.name = name;
084      this.claims = claims == null
085         ? Collections.emptyMap()
086         : Collections.unmodifiableMap(new LinkedHashMap<>(claims));
087   }
088
089   @Override /* Overridden from Principal */
090   public String getName() {
091      return name;
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * Returns an unmodifiable view of the full claim set.
096    *
097    * @return The full claim map. Never <jk>null</jk>.
098    */
099   public Map<String,Object> getClaims() {
100      return claims;
101   }
102
103   /**
104    * Returns the value of a claim by name with the supplied target type.
105    *
106    * <p>
107    * See the class-level Javadoc for the coercion contract.
108    *
109    * @param <T> The target type.
110    * @param claimName The claim name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
111    * @param type The target type. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
112    * @return The coerced value, or {@link Optional#empty()} if the claim is absent or cannot be coerced.
113    */
114   public <T> Optional<T> getClaim(String claimName, Class<T> type) {
115      assertArgNotNull("claimName", claimName);
116      assertArgNotNull("type", type);
117      var v = claims.get(claimName);
118      if (v == null)
119         return oe();
120      return o(coerce(v, type));
121   }
122
123   /**
124    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if a claim with the supplied name exists in the claim set.
125    *
126    * @param claimName The claim name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
127    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the claim is present, even if its value is the JSON {@code null} literal.
128    */
129   public boolean hasClaim(String claimName) {
130      assertArgNotNull("claimName", claimName);
131      return claims.containsKey(claimName);
132   }
133
134   @SuppressWarnings({
135      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: type parameter is verified by isInstance check before casting.
136   })
137   private static <T> T coerce(Object v, Class<T> type) {
138      if (type.isInstance(v))
139         return (T) v;
140      if (type == String.class)
141         return (T) v.toString();
142      if (v instanceof Number n) {
143         if (type == Integer.class || type == int.class)
144            return (T) Integer.valueOf(n.intValue());
145         if (type == Long.class || type == long.class)
146            return (T) Long.valueOf(n.longValue());
147         if (type == Double.class || type == double.class)
148            return (T) Double.valueOf(n.doubleValue());
149         if (type == Float.class || type == float.class)
150            return (T) Float.valueOf(n.floatValue());
151      }
152      return null;
153   }
154
155   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
156   public String toString() {
157      return "ClaimsPrincipal[name=" + name + ", claims=" + claims.keySet() + "]";
158   }
159}
